mnemonics: B.C. (Before Complexity) - NoSQL is used Before the Complexity of Big Data & high-velocity web applications.
keyPoints: NoSQL databases are non-relational, schema-less, and designed for horizontal scaling.
They are optimized for specific data models like key-value, document, column-family, and graph.
NoSQL systems are ideal for handling large volumes of unstructured or semi-structured data (Big Data).
commonMistakes: Assuming NoSQL means 'no SQL' at all; many support SQL-like query languages.
Thinking NoSQL is better than SQL in all cases; the choice depends entirely on the application's specific requirements (e.g., ACID transactions).
Believing that schema-less means no structure; data has implicit structure, but it's not enforced by the database until write time.
